```{r, eval = FALSE}
use_julia(nthreads = parallelly::availableCores() - 1)
```

or: 

```{r}
# future::plan(future::multisession, workers = parallelly::availableCores() - 1)
```

If not possible, regular simulation can still be used for the analyses below, albeit with longer runtimes.

### Establishing Boundary Conditions of Phenomena

Set up model for ensemble simulation:

```{r, fig.height=4}
# Simulation study
sfm0 <- sfm0 |> sim_specs(save_n = 100, 
   language = "julia",
   seed = 123) |>
  # Random initial conditions
  update(c(demands, resources, engagement, energy), eqn = runif(1, 0.01, 2))
sfm <- sfm0
```

Ensemble simulation with random initial conditions:

```{r}
sims <- ensemble(sfm, n = 1000)
plot(sims, vars = c("engagement", "demands"))
# plot(sims, vars = c("engagement", "energy", "demands"))
```

#### Parameter Variation

Vary `motivation_rate`: the rate at which resources increase engagement.

```{r, fig.height = 8}
n <- 100
motivation_rates = c(0.2, 0.7, 2) 

sims <- ensemble(sfm0,
  n = n,
  conditions = list(motivation_rate = motivation_rates),
  return_sims = TRUE, seed = 123
)

plot(sims,
  type = "sims", i = 1:n, alpha = .25,
  nrows = 3,
   central_tendency = FALSE, 
  vars = c("engagement", "demands")
  # vars = c("engagement", "energy", "demands")
)
```


```{r include=FALSE}
if (recreate_figs) {
  sfm_ens_par <- sfm
  n_ens_par <- n
}
```



### "What If" Scenarios: Developing Interventions

```{r}
# sfm <- sfm0 |>
#   constant(start, eqn = 21) |>
#   constant(input, eqn = pulse(times, start,
#    height = .15, width = 14)) |>
#   change_type(effort_rate, new_type = stock) |>
#   flow(intervention, from = effort_rate, 
#   eqn = effort_rate * input(t), 
#   label = "Intervention")

sfm <- sfm0 |>
  constant(start, eqn = 21) |>
  constant(duration, eqn = 14) |>
  constant(intervention, eqn = pulse(times, start, width = duration)) |>
  change_type(motivation_rate, new_type = stock) |>
  flow(intervention_effect, to = motivation_rate, 
  eqn = .05 * intervention(t), 
  label = "Intervention Effect")

# Specify seed, as not all initial conditions will show improvement from intervention
sim <- simulate(sfm, seed = 123)
plot(sim)
```



```{r}
# Simulation study
n <- 100
sims <- ensemble(sfm, n = n, return_sims = TRUE)
plot(sims,
  type = "sims", i = 1:n, alpha = .25, central_tendency = FALSE,
  vars = c("engagement", "demands", "motivation_rate")
)
```


```{r include=FALSE}
if (recreate_figs) {
  sfm_int <- sfm
}
```



To compute the effectiveness of the intervention, we increase the simulation length and only save the last timepoint.

```{r}
n <- 1000
sfm <- sfm |> sim_specs(stop = 1000, save_at = 1000)
sims <- ensemble(sfm, n = n, return_sims = TRUE)
df <- as.data.frame(sims, direction = "wide", type = "sims")
tab <- table(round(df$engagement, 0)) |>
  prop.table() |>
  as.data.frame()
colnames(tab) <- c("Engagement", "Proportion")
print(tab)
```

A shorter intervention is not effective.

```{r}
sfm2 <- update(sfm, duration, eqn = 7)
sims2 <- ensemble(sfm2, n = n, return_sims = TRUE)
df2 <- as.data.frame(sims2, direction = "wide", type = "sims")
tab2 <- table(round(df2$engagement, 0)) |>
  prop.table() |>
  as.data.frame()
colnames(tab2) <- c("Engagement", "Proportion")
print(tab2)
```


```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
if (recreate_figs) {
  show_vars <- c("engagement", "demands") |> rev()
  vars_ <- VARS[VARS %in% show_vars]
  colors_ <- COLORS[VARS %in% show_vars]
  ylim <- c(-0.1, 3)
  margin <- 0.05
  alpha <- .15
  annot_ <- utils::modifyList(annot, list(font = list(size = font_size_subplot + 1, family = font_family)))

  # Ensemble (plain)
  sfm <- sfm_ens_par |> sim_specs(save_at = dt)
  sims <- ensemble(sfm, n = 1000)

  pl_ens <- plot(sims,
    vars = vars_, colors = colors_,
    showlegend = FALSE,
    main = "", sub = "", ylim = ylim, xlab = ""
  ) |> rm_grid()

  # Parameter variation
  sims <- ensemble(sfm,
    n = n_ens_par,
    conditions = list(motivation_rate = motivation_rates),
     return_sims = TRUE
  )

  pl_par <- plot(sims,
    type = "sims", i = 1:n_ens_par, ylim = ylim,
    vars = vars_, colors = colors_, alpha = alpha,
    main = "", sub = "", shareY = TRUE,
    nrows = 3, central_tendency = FALSE, margin = margin,
    showlegend = FALSE, j_labels = FALSE, xlab = ""
  ) |> rm_grid()  |>
    # Remove grid lines
    plotly::layout(
      xaxis2 = list(showgrid = FALSE, showline = TRUE),
      yaxis2 = list(showgrid = FALSE, showline = TRUE, range = c(ylim[1], 3.5)),
      xaxis3 = list(showgrid = FALSE, showline = TRUE),
      yaxis3 = list(showgrid = FALSE, showline = TRUE, range = c(ylim[1], 3.5))
    )
  pl_par

  # Increase spacing between subplots
  # pl_par$x$layout$margin <- list(t = 75, b = 75, l = 75, r = 75)
  # pl_par$x$layoutAttrs[[1]]$margin <- list(t = 100, b = 100, l = 100, r = 100)

  # pl$x$layoutAttrs[[1]]$margin


  # Intervention
  show_vars <- c("engagement", "demands", "motivation_rate") |> rev()
  vars_ <- VARS[VARS %in% show_vars]
  colors_ <- COLORS[VARS %in% show_vars]

  sfm <- sfm_int |> sim_specs(save_at = dt)
  sims <- ensemble(sfm, n = n_ens_par, only_stocks = FALSE, return_sims = TRUE)

  pl_int <- plot(sims,
    type = "sims", i = 1:n_ens_par,
    alpha = alpha, central_tendency = FALSE,
    # type = "summary",
    showlegend = TRUE,
    vars = vars_, colors = colors_, ylim = ylim,
    main = "", sub = "", 
    wrap_width = 40
  ) |> rm_grid()
    
  pl_int

  # Plot thicker line for motivation_rate
  col_ <- COLORS[VARS == "motivation_rate"]
  pl_int <- pl_int |>
    plotly::add_trace(
      data = sims$df[sims$df$variable == "motivation_rate", , drop = FALSE],
      x = ~time,
      y = ~value,
      line = list(
        color = col_,
        linewidth = 7
      ),
      showlegend = FALSE,
      split = ~ interaction(variable, i), # ensures each line is treated separately
      type = "scatter",
      mode = "lines"
    )

  x1 <- 0.23
  x2 <- 0.77
  y1 <- 1.04
  y2 <- .5
  y3 <- .66
  y4 <- .32

  pl <- plotly::subplot(
    plotly::subplot(pl_ens, pl_int,
      shareX = TRUE,
      shareY = TRUE,
      titleY = FALSE,
      titleX = FALSE,
      margin = margin,
      # margin = c(0.025, 0.05, 0.025, 0.025),
      nrows = 2
    ),
    pl_par,
    shareX = FALSE,
    shareY = FALSE,
    titleY = FALSE,
    titleX = FALSE,
    margin = margin,
    # margin = c(0.05, 0.025, 0.025, 0.025),
    nrows = 1, widths = c(.5, .5)
  ) |>
    plotly::layout(
      title = list(text = ""),
      font = annot_font,
      legend = utils::modifyList(annot_legend, list(y = -0.14))
    ) |>
    plotly::layout(
      annotations = list(
        utils::modifyList(annot_xlab, list(y = -0.07)),
        utils::modifyList(annot_, list(x = x1, y = y1, text = "<i>A. Initial Condition Variation</i>")),
        utils::modifyList(annot_, list(x = x1, y = y2, text = "<i>E. Intervention on Motivation Rate</i>")),
        utils::modifyList(annot_, list(x = x2, y = y1, text = "<i>B. Parameter Variation: Burnout</i>")),
        utils::modifyList(annot_, list(x = x2, y = y3, text = "<i>C. Parameter Variation: Bistability</i>")),
        utils::modifyList(annot_, list(x = x2, y = y4, text = "<i>D. Parameter Variation: Healthy</i>"))
      )
    )

  export_plot(pl, file.path(filepath_figs, "learning_JDR.pdf"),
    width = 5.5, height = 5.5
  )

  sfm <- sfm |> sim_specs(save_at = 1)
}
```



### Informing Experimental and Statistical Design

Fit cross-lagged panel model to simulated data.

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(lavaan)
library(semPlot)

vars0 <- c("energy", "demands", "engagement", "resources", "performance")

# View constant values
df <- as.data.frame(sfm0, type = "constant", properties = "eqn")

# Update all constants to runif()
create_runif <- function(x, factor = 0.5){
  eqn <- paste0("runif(1, ", max(c(0, x - x * factor)), ", ", x + x * factor, ")")
  return(eqn)
}

# All constants can be updated at once by passing a dataframe to `update()`, which is more efficient than updating each constant separately.
# Adjust dataframe
df[["eqn"]] <- sapply(df$eqn, function(x) {
  if (grepl("^[0-9.]+$", x)) {
    create_runif(as.numeric(x))
  } else {
    x
  }
})

print(df)

sfm <- sfm0 |> update(df = df)

# # Simulation study
# sfm <- sfm0 |> 
#   # Bistable parameters
#   update(effort_rate, eqn = runif(1, 0.05, 0.6)) |>
#   update(motivation_rate, eqn = runif(1, .2, 1)) |>
#   update(m_slope, eqn = runif(1, 2, 4)) |>
#   update(engagement_decay_rate, eqn = runif(1, 0.05, 0.3)) |>
#   update(proactive_rate, eqn = runif(1, 0.1, 0.5)) |>
#   update(resource_decay_rate, eqn = runif(1, 0.1, 0.4)) |>
#   update(exo_resource_rate, eqn = runif(1, 0.01, 0.2)) |>
#   update(recovery_rate, eqn = runif(1, 0.3, 0.7)) |>
#   update(s_slope, eqn = runif(1, 4, 6)) |>
#   update(work_rate, eqn = runif(1, 0.25, .75)) |>
#   update(e_slope, eqn = runif(1, 8, 12)) |>
#   update(exo_demand_rate, eqn = runif(1, 0.05, 0.2)) |>
#   update(undermining_rate, eqn = runif(1, 3, 7))

sfm <- sfm |>
  sim_specs(
    save_at = 1,
    # save_at = c(20, 60, 100), stop = 100,
    # save_at = seq(0, 200, by = 1),
     stop = 200,
  language = "julia", only_stocks = FALSE,
  # language = "R",
    seed = 123 # ensemble seed
    ) |>
  # Random initial conditions
  update(c(demands, resources, engagement, energy), eqn = runif(1, 0.01, 2))

n <- 10000

sims <- ensemble(sfm, n = n, return_sims = TRUE)
plot(sims, vars = vars0, type = "summary")
plot(sims, vars = vars0, type = "sims", i = 1:100)
```


```{r}
# Run cross-lagged panel analysis between energy and demands for different lags
wave1 <- 20
# x1 <- "energy"
x1 <- "engagement"
x2 <- "demands"
lags <- seq(10, as.numeric(sfm$sim_specs$stop) - wave1, by = 1)
df <- as.data.frame(sims, direction = "wide", type = "sims")
dfX <- df[, c("i", "time", x1)] 
dfD <- df[, c("i", "time", x2)] 
dfX_wave1 <- dfX[dfX$time == wave1, setdiff(names(dfX), "time")]
colnames(dfX_wave1) <- gsub(x1, paste0(x1, "_wave1"), colnames(dfX_wave1))
dfD_wave1 <- dfD[dfD$time == wave1, setdiff(names(dfD), "time")]
colnames(dfD_wave1) <- gsub(x2, paste0(x2, "_wave1"), colnames(dfD_wave1))

model <- sprintf(" # wave 1 -> wave 2
  %s_wave2  ~ 1 + %s_wave1 + %s_wave1
  %s_wave2  ~ 1 + %s_wave1 + %s_wave1

  # concurrent covariances
  %s_wave1  ~~ %s_wave1
  %s_wave2  ~~ %s_wave2
", x1, x1, x2, x2, x1, x2, x1, x2, x1, x2) 
```


```{r}
# Plot SEM plot for one lag
lag <- chosen_lag <- 90

# Create wide format data for lavaan
dfX_wave2 <- dfX[dfX$time == (wave1 + lag), setdiff(names(dfX), "time")]
colnames(dfX_wave2) <- gsub(x1, paste0(x1, "_wave2"), colnames(dfX_wave2))
dfD_wave2 <- dfD[dfD$time == (wave1 + lag), setdiff(names(dfD), "time")]
colnames(dfD_wave2) <- gsub(x2, paste0(x2, "_wave2"), colnames(dfD_wave2))

data_wide <- dfX_wave1 |>
  dplyr::left_join(dfX_wave2, by = "i") |>
  dplyr::left_join(dfD_wave1, by = "i") |>
  dplyr::left_join(dfD_wave2, by = "i")

# Fit lavaan model
m1 <- lavaan::sem(model, data = data_wide, 
# robust to non-normality
estimator = "MLR")
          
# # summary(m1, standardized = TRUE) 
# parameterEstimates(m1, standardized = TRUE, boot.ci.type = "bca.simple")

# # Fit statistics:
# fitMeasures(m1, c("chisq", "df", "pvalue", "cfi", "rmsea", "srmr", "bic"))

# # chisq should be non-significant, but is often significant in large samples
# # rmsea < 0.01, 0.05, and 0.08 to indicate excellent, good, and mediocre fit, respectively.
# # srmr < .08 is good, < .10 is acceptable
# # lower bic is better


# modificationindices(m1) %>%
#   as_tibble() %>%
#   filter(mi>4) %>%
#   arrange(-mi)

# # Plot
# semPaths(m1, whatLabels = "std",
# #  layout = "spring", 
# #  edge.color = "black", sizeMan = 5, sizeLat = 7, nCharNodes = 0
# )


# Standardized parameter estimates (with standardized SEs/tests/CIs)
m1_est <- chosen_lag_est <- lavaan::standardizedSolution(
  m1,
  type = "std.all",
  se = TRUE,
  pvalue = TRUE,
  ci = TRUE
)

label_col <- "est.std"
label_digits <- 2

semPlot::semPaths(m1,
  whatLabels = "std",
  edge.label.cex = 1.2,
  edge.color = "black",
  sizeMan = 5, sizeLat = 7, nCharNodes = 0
  # layout = "spring"
)

```

```{r}
# Storage
results <- vector("list", length(lags))

# Loop over lags
for (lag in lags) {

  # Create wide format data for lavaan
  dfX_wave2 <- dfX[dfX$time == (wave1 + lag), setdiff(names(dfX), "time")] 
  colnames(dfX_wave2) <- gsub(x1, paste0(x1, "_wave2"), colnames(dfX_wave2))
  dfD_wave2 <- dfD[dfD$time == (wave1 + lag), setdiff(names(dfD), "time")]
  colnames(dfD_wave2) <- gsub(x2, paste0(x2, "_wave2"), colnames(dfD_wave2))

  data_wide <- dfX_wave1 |>
    dplyr::left_join(dfX_wave2, by = "i") |>
    dplyr::left_join(dfD_wave1, by = "i") |>
    dplyr::left_join(dfD_wave2, by = "i")

  # Fit lavaan model
  m1 <- lavaan::sem(model, data = data_wide, estimator = "MLR", # robust to non-normality
          #  se = "bootstrap", bootstrap = 5000
           )
  # summary(m1, standardized = TRUE) 

  # Store standardized parameter estimates (including standardized SEs and CIs)
  out <- lavaan::standardizedSolution(
    m1,
    type = "std.all",
    se = TRUE,
    pvalue = TRUE,
    ci = TRUE
  ) 
  out <- out[out[["op"]] == "~", c("lhs", "rhs", 
  "est.std", "se", "ci.lower", "ci.upper")]
  out[["lag"]] <- lag
  out[["est"]] <- out[["est.std"]]
  out[["est.std"]] <- NULL
  out <- out[, c("lhs", "rhs", "est", "se", "ci.lower", "ci.upper", "lag")]
  results[[as.character(lag)]] <- out

}

# Plot
results_df <- as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, results))
results_df[["path"]] <- paste(results_df[["lhs"]], results_df[["rhs"]], sep = " ~ ")
results_df <- results_df[, c("path", "est", "se", "ci.lower", "ci.upper", "lag")]
rownames(results_df) <- NULL
head(results_df, n = 10)


# Sort paths for maximum lag
results_max_lag <- results_df[results_df$lag == max(results_df$lag), ]
results_max_lag[order(results_max_lag$est, decreasing = TRUE), ]

# Chosen lag
results_df[results_df$lag == chosen_lag, ]


```

```{r}
# Relabel dataframe
label_map <- c(
  sprintf("%s (wave 2) ~ %s (wave 1)", stringr::str_to_title(x2), stringr::str_to_title(x1)),
  sprintf("%s (wave 2) ~ %s (wave 1)", stringr::str_to_title(x1), stringr::str_to_title(x2)),
  sprintf("%s (wave 2) ~ %s (wave 1)", stringr::str_to_title(x2), stringr::str_to_title(x2)),
  sprintf("%s (wave 2) ~ %s (wave 1)", stringr::str_to_title(x1), stringr::str_to_title(x1))
)

names(label_map) <- c(
  paste0(x2, "_wave2 ~ ", x1, "_wave1"),
  paste0(x1, "_wave2 ~ ", x2, "_wave1"),
  paste0(x2, "_wave2 ~ ", x2, "_wave1"),
  paste0(x1, "_wave2 ~ ", x1, "_wave1")
)

results_df$path <- as.factor(label_map[results_df$path])

# colors <- c(
#   COLORS[VARS == "performance"],
#   COLORS[VARS == "engagement"],
#   COLORS[VARS == "motivation_rate"],
#   COLORS[VARS == "resources"]
# )# |> rev()
colors <- c(
  COLORS[VARS == "engagement"],
  COLORS[VARS == "performance"],
  COLORS[VARS == "resources"],
  COLORS[VARS == "motivation_rate"]
)
names(colors) <- unname(label_map)
scales::show_col(colors)

# Plot lag-dependent parameter estimates with confidence intervals
pl <- plotly::plot_ly(results_df, x = ~lag, y = ~est, 
color = ~path, split = ~path, legendgroup = ~path,
    colors = colors,
    type = 'scatter', mode = 'lines',
        line = list(),
        showlegend = TRUE) |> 
  #       # Add vertical rectangle for chosen lag
  #       plotly::layout(
  # shapes = list(
  #   list(
  #     type = "rect",
  #     x0 = chosen_lag - 0.5, x1 = chosen_lag + 0.5,
  #     y0 = 0, y1 = 1,
  #     yref = "paper",
  #      fillcolor = "#DDD9C3",
  #     # opacity = 0.7,
  #     line = list(width = 0),
  #       layer = "below"
  #     # line = list(color = COLORS[VARS == "demands"], 
  #     # width = 2
  #     # dash = "dash"
  #     )
  #   )
  # ) |>
   plotly::add_ribbons(x = ~lag, ymin = ~ci.lower, 
   ymax = ~ci.upper, color = ~path, split = ~path,
    line = list(color = 'transparent'),
            showlegend = FALSE) |>
    plotly::layout(
      showlegend = TRUE,
      title = "",
      xaxis = list(
        title = "Time between waves (days)",
        font = list(size = font_size)
      ),
      yaxis = list(
        title = "Parameter estimate (standardized)",
        font = list(size = font_size)
      ),
      font = annot_font
    ) |> rm_grid() |>
    plotly::layout(
      xaxis = list(tickvals = list(10, 50, 90, 130, 170)),
      font = annot_font,
      # legend = utils::modifyList(annot_legend, list(x = 0.5, y = -0.3))
      legend = utils::modifyList(annot_legend, list(x = 1.7, y = 0.6))
    )  


if (recreate_figs) {
  export_plot(pl,
    file.path(filepath_figs, "JDR_SEM_across_lags.pdf"),
    # width = 3.5, height = 2.5
    width = 7, height = 3
  )
}

```

## Session Information

```{r echo=TRUE}
sessionInfo()
```
